meta.description: Private reference data for APAC AI Intelligence site

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic cumulative collected revenue ~$5B (all-time through 2024/early 2025)
dataPoints.notes: Sworn court affidavit — highest confidence. This is cumulative collected revenue, not ARR. Used to calibrate Anthropic's 2025 collected revenue estimate.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic 2025 annualised revenue run rate ~$4.5B
dataPoints.notes: The Information reporting. Cross-checked against sworn affidavit: if cumulative ever is $5B, then 2025 collected could be $3.5-4.5B depending on ramp. We used $4.5B as the Sankey provider inflow from customers.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI H1 2024 revenue ~$2.27B (derived from Microsoft revenue share)
dataPoints.notes: Zitron obtained Microsoft internal docs showing $454.7M H1 2024 revenue share from OpenAI. At 20% rev share → ~$2.27B OpenAI H1 revenue. Annualised with growth → $6B 2025 collected estimate.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI 2025 collected revenue estimate ~$6B
dataPoints.notes: Derived from Zitron's H1 2024 data ($2.27B H1) + reported growth trajectory. NOT the $12.7B exit ARR figure widely reported. Actual collected likely $5-7B range. $6B is mid-estimate. Total OpenAI inflow on Sankey is $12B ($6B customer + $6B VC).

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI operating loss ~$6B in 2025
dataPoints.notes: Multiple reports indicate OpenAI losing ~$5-6B annually. Used $6B as VC subsidy in Sankey (VC/Investors → OpenAI flow).

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic operating loss ~$5.2B in 2025
dataPoints.notes: Anthropic total system cost estimated at $9.7B (inference + people/SG&A). Customer revenue $4.5B. Gap = $5.2B funded by VC rounds (Amazon, Google, etc). Sankey: VC → Anthropic = $5.2B.

dataPoints.claim: Total AI customer revenue (global, 2025) ~$15.6B
dataPoints.notes: Sum of all customer flows: Consumer $4.5B + SME $3B + Enterprise $8.1B = $15.6B. This is actual collected revenue for inference services, NOT ARR and NOT including training capex.

dataPoints.claim: VC operating subsidy to AI providers ~$12B in 2025
dataPoints.notes: The gap between customer revenue ($15.6B) and total operating costs ($27.6B). Breakdown: OpenAI $6B + Anthropic $5.2B + IaaS $0.3B + Google $0.5B. This is purely the operating gap, NOT training capex or GPU procurement.

dataPoints.claim: Inference compute costs ~$13B (47% of total AI system spend)
dataPoints.notes: Sum of all provider → Inference flows: OpenAI $7.2B (60%) + Anthropic $4.2B (43%) + IaaS $0.4B (50%) + Google $1.2B (48%) = $13B. NVIDIA captures majority of this via GPU pricing.

dataPoints.claim: Traditional SaaS AI features revenue ~$2.8B (2025)
dataPoints.sourceAuthor: APAC AI Intel (derived from deep analysis)
dataPoints.notes: Revenue from AI SKUs sold by traditional SaaS vendors: Microsoft M365 Copilot, Salesforce Einstein GPT, ServiceNow Now Assist, etc. Previously validated through deep analysis. Zitron is skeptical but doesn't have new data points beyond what we already calculated. Kept at $2.8B.

dataPoints.claim: Microsoft M365 Copilot H1 2024 revenue share to OpenAI: $454.7M
dataPoints.notes: From Microsoft internal documents obtained by Ed Zitron. This is the revenue share paid BY Microsoft TO OpenAI for inference, not Microsoft's total Copilot revenue. At ~20% rev share implies ~$2.27B OpenAI revenue from Microsoft channel alone in H1 2024.

dataPoints.claim: Hyperscaler cloud margin on AI model serving ~20%
dataPoints.notes: AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, Google Vertex take ~20% margin on pass-through model serving. They use their own infrastructure (circular economics) and pay model providers for inference. Exact margin not publicly disclosed. $0.4B of $2B Cloud Channel flows to Margin.

dataPoints.claim: IaaS/open model provider gross margin 15-35%
dataPoints.notes: IaaS providers (Together AI, Fireworks, OpenRouter) have a thin positive gross margin on inference — better than closed frontier models which are negative. But zero operating margin — GM is entirely consumed by people and ops. Range based on infrastructure business comparables. Insufficient public data for higher confidence.

dataPoints.claim: Open models trail frontier by ~3 months
dataPoints.notes: Llama, DeepSeek, Qwen releases consistently follow frontier model capabilities by approximately 3 months. This gap has been narrowing over time.

dataPoints.claim: Llama downloads exceed 1.2B
dataPoints.notes: Meta's official Llama download count. Used in the 'shift to open models' narrative section.

dataPoints.claim: Open models account for 61% of OpenRouter token volume
dataPoints.notes: From OpenRouter's public rankings/statistics. Shows open models dominate by volume even if closed models lead on revenue per query.

dataPoints.claim: Mag 7 AI capex ~$250B+ in 2025
dataPoints.notes: Combined AI-related capital expenditure from Apple, Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta, NVIDIA, Tesla. Used in 'What's NOT in this chart' section to show the scale of investment vs actual revenue.

dataPoints.claim: Model training compute spend ~$30-50B in 2025
dataPoints.notes: Estimated total spend on training runs across all major labs. Extremely opaque — no one publishes training costs. Used in 'What's NOT in this chart' section.

dataPoints.claim: Total VC investment into AI ~$150B+ in 2025
dataPoints.notes: Total venture capital deployed into AI companies in 2025. Includes both operating capital and training capex. Used in 'What's NOT in this chart' to show $15:$1 investment-to-revenue ratio.

dataPoints.claim: Token and subscription prices need to rise ~55% to achieve 30% gross margin
dataPoints.notes: At current inference costs ($13B) and customer revenue ($15.6B), achieving 30% GM requires revenue of ~$18.6B. That's a 55% increase on current customer revenue minus SaaS margin. Used in sustainability path #3.

dataPoints.claim: Inference costs need to fall ~35% for minimally viable 30% gross margin
dataPoints.notes: Inference costs need to drop from ~$13B to ~$8.4B for 30% gross margin at current revenue. Used in sustainability path #1.

dataPoints.claim: Shift of ~20-30% of workloads to open models reaches system breakeven
dataPoints.notes: Open models are 5-10x cheaper per token. Moving 20-30% of current closed-model workloads to open models via IaaS providers would bring the overall system to breakeven without hardware cost reduction. Used in sustainability path #2.

dataPoints.claim: AI token prices falling ~80% year-on-year
dataPoints.notes: Observable from public API pricing across all major providers. GPT-4 class models went from ~$30/1M tokens to ~$3-5/1M tokens in 18 months. Used in sustainability path #3 narrative about the tension between needing higher prices and market reality.

dataPoints.claim: Only SaaS + hyperscaler margin exists in the system: $2.15B
dataPoints.notes: Total margin in the Sankey system. Comes from: Trad SaaS vendors $1.75B (Microsoft, Salesforce, ServiceNow keep 62% of their $2.8B) + Hyperscaler cloud pass-through margin $0.4B (20% of $2B). Frontier providers and IaaS providers contribute zero margin.

dataPoints.claim: For every $1 customers spend on AI, investors spend $15+ building it
dataPoints.notes: Customer revenue $15.6B vs total investment $250B+ = roughly 16:1 ratio. Rounded to $15:$1. Used in 'What's NOT in this chart' section.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic hit $14B ARR in February 2026, grown from $1B in 14 months (12x YoY)
dataPoints.notes: Corroborating — citing widely reported figures. Brad Gerstner (investor) did not dispute.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic had a $6B revenue month in February 2026 (28-day month)
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative — direct investor with board-level access. Confirms $14B ARR figure.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic valued at $380B (February 2026)
dataPoints.notes: Corroborating — citing reported valuation.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI ended 2025 at $20B ARR, grown from $2B to $20B in 24 months
dataPoints.notes: Corroborating — multiple reports. Dashboard updated from $16.8B.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI valued at $840B (February 2026)
dataPoints.notes: Corroborating — citing reported valuation.

dataPoints.claim: Sarah Friar (Anthropic CFO): every gigawatt = $10B annual revenue
dataPoints.sourceAuthor: Chamath Palihapitiya citing Sarah Friar (Anthropic CFO)
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative — from Anthropic CFO directly. Key metric for DC economics.

dataPoints.claim: Data center all-in cost: $50B per gigawatt (powered shell, land, permits, infrastructure)
dataPoints.sourceAuthor: Chamath Palihapitiya (first-hand, his own DC project in Arizona)
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative — first-hand operator building 1GW DC. Cost went from $10B to $50B.

dataPoints.claim: Data center payback: 5-year break-even, profit in years 6-8
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative — from his own DC economics analysis.

dataPoints.claim: Compute constraint at record high — more constrained than any time in last 3 years
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative — direct investor with visibility into capacity.

dataPoints.claim: NVIDIA invested $40B in OpenAI + Anthropic, expects both to IPO in 2026
dataPoints.notes: Corroborating — Jensen public statement that this is his last private investment.

dataPoints.claim: No proven enterprise ROI from AI at scale — zero examples of sustained positive margin expansion
dataPoints.sourceAuthor: Chamath Palihapitiya (first-hand, runs companies consuming AI)
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative bear case — heavy AI consumer seeing no revenue gains despite tripling token spend.

dataPoints.claim: Token spend tripling every 3 months with no corresponding revenue gains
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative — his own company experience. Key bear case datapoint.

dataPoints.claim: 40% of protested US data centers get canceled — ~7GW at risk (Q1 2026), ~$70B/yr revenue at risk
dataPoints.notes: Indicative — uses Sarah Friar $10B/GW multiplier on canceled capacity.

dataPoints.claim: 85% of startup tokens now on open source models — every startup standing up local models
dataPoints.notes: Indicative — anecdotal from well-connected operator. If true, validates the open model volume story.

dataPoints.claim: Meta spending $115-135B on AI infrastructure in 2026
dataPoints.sourceAuthor: Alex Kantrowitz citing Meta public capex guidance
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative — Meta own public guidance. Midpoint $125B.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI considering IPO as soon as Q4 2026
dataPoints.sourceAuthor: Alex Kantrowitz citing Wall Street Journal
dataPoints.notes: Corroborating — WSJ source. Aligns with Jensen/Gerstner IPO comments.

dataPoints.claim: Microsoft stock 30% off its high (March 2026)
dataPoints.notes: Corroborating — verifiable market data.

dataPoints.claim: Add Fathom to Top Token Consumers (~7B/day)
dataPoints.notes: Auto-applied from claims review. Weight: authoritative. Dedup: ?.
